wikidata_candidates.label: Béla Bartók
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe different individuals. The card refers to Stephen Bartok, a Mechanical Engineer at Litton Industries (a company founded in 1953). The Wikidata entity is Béla Bartók, the famous Hungarian composer who died in 1945. They share a surname and a connection to New York City, but their professions and timelines are incompatible.

wikidata_org_candidates.label: Litton Industries
wikidata_org_candidates.description: defense contractor in the United States
wikidata_org_candidates.reasoning: The name 'Litton Industries' on the card matches the Wikidata entity exactly. The professional profile of the individual on the card (Mechanical Engineer in electronics and high-speed moving objects) is highly consistent with the Wikidata description of Litton Industries as an aerospace and defense contractor.

wikidata_org_candidates.label: Litton Industries bombing
wikidata_org_candidates.description: 1982 bombing of a nuclear weapons component production facility
wikidata_org_candidates.reasoning: The Wikidata entity (Q6653219) refers to a specific historical event, namely the 1982 bombing of a facility, whereas the card refers to 'Litton Industries' as an organization and employer.
